title: thymus caucasicus subsp. grossheimii
intro: Thymus caucasicus subsp. grossheimii, also known as Caucasian thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that is native to the Caucasus region. It is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with fragrant leaves and purple flowers, prized for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Caucasian thyme, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
